title: gnaphalium faetidum
intro: Gnaphalium faetidum, also known as stinking chamomile, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), characterized by its distinctive unpleasant odor. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Gnaphalium faetidum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
